mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9 percent signals severe labor market slack that suppresses household consumption and corporate revenue growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of months where UNRATE remains above 9.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and new housing starts confirms broad-based consumer and builder confidence, creating a positive feedback loop that sustains equity market gains over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates tightening capacity utilization and rising corporate output, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive equity returns within the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following positive INDPRO momentum, or the signal reverses direction during acute liquidity crunches.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero policy rates and declining unemployment confirms successful monetary transmission to the real economy, driving risk-on positioning and positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for inflation expectations in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent signals insufficient aggregate demand to sustain corporate profit margins, prompting portfolio de-risking and negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of quarters following sub-1% GDP growth, or the signal fails to predict direction in a walk-forward validation framework.

mechanism_narrative: A stable and moderate consumer price index reflects healthy demand pricing power without triggering aggressive central bank tightening, supporting nominal earnings growth and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months when CPIAUCSL growth remains between 1.5% and 2.5%, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls exceeding 130 million indicate robust labor market absorption that directly fuels consumer spending and corporate revenue, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of triggered months, or the predictive power disappears when adjusted for seasonal employment fluctuations.
